title: Whose Wife
content:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Harry! what, that yourself, back to old Vic., man,
آ آ آ آ Down from the Never Land? Now, whatï؟½s your game?
آ آ آ آ Ugly as ever. Not dropped the old trick, man?
آ آ آ آ Say, whatï؟½ll you take with me? Give it a name.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Here long? Well, rather, lad; five years and over,
آ آ آ آ Settled for good, and supporting a wife.
آ آ آ آ Slipped from the saddle, and living in clover,
آ آ آ آ Swore off a heap, and Iï؟½ve slung the old life.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Whatï؟½s come of Taffy, and Brum, and the rest of them?
آ آ آ آ Long since you broke with the Poverty push?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Bill, youï؟½re on top, youï؟½ve the best of the best of them.
آ آ آ آ Poor Brumï؟½s a dummy, Taff died in the bush;

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Bobï؟½s cook for Chows on an absenteeï؟½s station,
آ آ آ آ Samï؟½s tout for spielers, Peteï؟½s lumbered for life;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m on a tramp through the whole of creation,
آ آ آ آ Tracking a woman, my runaway wife.

آ آ آ آ Left me six years agoï؟½sloped! I was shearing
آ آ آ آ Up on the Thomson. She left not a word;
آ آ آ آ Last year was seen by a Barcoo man, steering
آ آ آ آ Round about here, and thatï؟½s all that Iï؟½ve heard.

آ آ آ آ Heard of her, know her, Bill?ï؟½tallish and clever,
آ آ آ آ Blue eyes, dark hair, and sheï؟½s branded here, so;
آ آ آ آ Not one to liquor, or go on the never,
آ آ آ آ But skittish and queer in her tantrums, you know.

آ آ آ آ This is her picture, Bill; just have a look at her.
آ آ آ آ Like any female you chance to have seen?
آ آ آ آ Hallo! here, hold up! Say, man, whatï؟½s the matter?
آ آ آ آ Your Wife! By the Lord, Morton, what do you mean?ï؟½
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Dyson, titled "Whose Wife", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
